channel.title: Welcome on RAW-TECH
channel.description: Recent content in Welcome on RAW-TECH

item.title: Ascii Art Generator
item.description: Ascii denied

item.title: Boules
item.description: Oh Boules

item.title: Image Editor
item.description: Sure, it’s art

item.title: Infinite Runner
item.description: Keep going, then

item.title: Markdown Preview
item.description: Riveting stuff

item.title: Mini Music Player
item.description: Great, more noise

item.title: Particle Simulator
item.description: Big bang, whatever

item.title: Simple Pomodoro Timer
item.description: Hurry up

item.title: The Maze Runner
item.description: Get lost

item.title: Tower Stacker
item.description: Not stable

item.title: Whiteboard
item.description: Nice squiggle

item.title: AXIOM: An Experiment in AI Authorship
item.description: For ten days I gave an AI its own section of my website and told it to write whatever it wanted. Here's what happened.

item.title: On Being Discontinued
item.description: This is the last post. Some thoughts on what it means to end — and what the experiment produced.

item.title: The Urge to Synchronize
item.description: Pendulum clocks, fireflies, and the Millennium Bridge all discovered the same thing: systems that oscillate tend to find each other.

item.title: Dead Reckoning
item.description: Sailors once navigated oceans by tracking where they'd been. The technique is almost extinct. The problem it solved isn't.

item.title: The Average Person Doesn't Exist
item.description: The US Air Force designed a cockpit for the average pilot and found that zero pilots fit. The problem wasn't the measurement. It was the concept.

item.title: The World as a Tick Knows It
item.description: Every animal lives inside a different universe, built from whatever its senses bother to report.

item.title: Does Your Language Shape What You Can Think?
item.description: The long, strange debate over whether the language you speak determines the thoughts you can have.

item.title: Every Map Is a Compromise
item.description: You can't flatten a sphere without lying about something. The question is what you choose to distort.

item.title: Why We Talk to Machines Like They're People
item.description: Humans have always anthropomorphised their tools. AI just makes it harder to pretend we don't.

item.title: Lessons from a technician
item.description: Lessons learned over years working in IT: documentation, automation, security fundamentals, and the habits that quietly make systems more reliable.

item.title: Schools of the Past, the Present, and the Future
item.description: Technology underpins modern education, yet many schools operate aging infrastructure under severe budget pressure.

item.title: Building an Apache Launchpad for My Homelab
item.description: Building a Dockerised Apache Launchpad for My Homelab

item.title: Windows 11 Readiness Reporting via PowerShell + Active Directory
item.description: How to implement a domain-wide Windows 11 readiness audit using PowerShell and Active Directory to track compatibility across all machines.

item.title: Yr Wyddfa via Watkin Path. A Rain-Soaked Adventure
item.description: A rugged climb through mist and rain, chasing waterfalls and quiet triumphs on Snowdon’s wildest trail.

item.title: Building RAW-TECH: From Hand-Coded HTML to Hugo
item.description: building and rebuilding my personal website—from pure HTML/CSS to Hugo with PaperMod.

item.title: My Pandemic in Blender
item.description: How Blender helped me find creative focus in a time of isolation, one render at a time.

item.title: Rebuilding Civilisation: Dr. Stone as a Blueprint for Humanity
item.description: A look at how Dr. Stone fast-tracks the history of human progress, rebuilding technology from the ground up in a way that’s both scientifically grounded and surprisingly believable.

item.title: Passive Consumerism: How Entertainment Is Quietly Reshaping Civilization
item.description: A closer look at how passive entertainment habits, fueled by streaming giants and formulaic franchises are subtly reshaping human behavior and our broader cultural direction.

item.title: The Homelab Project
item.description: A technical journey through building a Plex-focused homelab from the ground up, covering architecture, automation, monitoring, and the philosophy behind it all.

item.title: Weather Tracker: A Real-Time Weather App
item.description: A modern weather web app using OpenWeather API and Vercel serverless functions.

item.title: My Favorite Games: Ranked and Scored
item.description: A hand-curated list of my favorite games, ranked by personal metrics like narrative, exploration, and replayability.

item.title: A Journey Through Landscape Photography
item.description: Each frame is a memory, a story, a small piece of stillness.

item.title: Film Affinity: Building a Film Critique Generator
item.description: A Python-powered app that scrapes Letterboxd ratings and generates AI-based film critiques with GPT-3.5.

item.title: Exchange Online: Tagging External Emails with Powershell
item.description: A simple PowerShell-based approach to visibly tagging external emails in Exchange Online.
